title: Breaker Panel Load Balancing
dataSources: Normalize three-phase branch or panelboard metering into `index=power` as `sourcetype="panel:phase"` with extracted fields `phase_a_amps`, `phase_b_amps`, `phase_c_amps`, plus `panel_id` and `rated_amps` so the imbalance math in the catalog SPL resolves. Typical paths mirror the Panelboard metering and PQ analyzer context in App: poll current transducer or meter outputs through SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app ID 1537) or Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app ID 6266) with `inputs.conf` trap or poll stanzas, push sub-minute aggregates through `http://<heavy-forwarder>:8088/services/collector/event` HEC tokens, or run a scripted modular input that reads the same values the PQ tool already publishes to CSV.
spl:
index=power sourcetype="panel:phase"
| eval max_p=max(phase_a_amps, phase_b_amps, phase_c_amps), min_p=min(phase_a_amps, phase_b_amps, phase_c_amps), imbalance=max_p-min_p
| where imbalance > rated_amps*0.15
| table panel_id, phase_a_amps, phase_b_amps, phase_c_amps, imbalance
description: Flags sustained three-phase amp divergence at branch panels when Splunk rows tagged `sourcetype="panel:phase"` show the spread between highest and lowest phase RMS currents exceeding your chosen fraction of each panel's rated frame amps.
value: Keeping phases evenly loaded reduces neutral conductor heating, breaker contact wear, and nuisance trips in dense single-phase rack rows, so facility teams extend equipment life and protect uptime commitments without waiting for a physical panel walk-through.
implementation: Normalize branch-board CT or PQ-feed currents into `panel:phase`, maintain accurate `rated_amps` per `panel_id`, schedule the imbalance SPL every five minutes against `-5m`, tune the imbalance percentage macro for dense single-phase racks, and correlate alerts with rack migration tickets before dispatching electricians.
visualization: Primary: clustered column chart comparing `phase_a_amps`, `phase_b_amps`, `phase_c_amps` per `panel_id` for the latest five-minute slice. Secondary: table sorted by descending `imbalance` with color rules. Tertiary: single-value KPI counting panels breaching threshold in the selected window with drill-down to raw events.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Set up and use HTTP Event Collector
Splunk Lantern — operations best practices library

knownFalsePositives: Scenario A — Overnight SAN boot storms swing only one phase on PDU-fed racks for ten minutes; distinguish genuine imbalance from benign bursts by requiring the condition across two consecutive five-minute buckets or by joining a `rack_maintenance.csv` lookup keyed on panel_id. Scenario B — Quarterly generator load banks purposefully shed one leg; suppress using a `facility_dr_window` lookup tied to Change records so events still index but alerts hush. Scenario C — SNMP walk restarts after firmware upgrades replay identical amp triplets with skewed `_time`; dedup on panel_id plus the rounded phase tuple within sixty seconds before scoring. Scenario D — Harmonic-rich VFD startups temporarily distort one CT while the magnetic trip is safe; blend in a PQ analyzer total harmonic distortion field if present and raise thresholds when THD exceeds a documented ceiling instead of paging on amps alone.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Confirm the ingestion path tied to Panelboard metering and PQ analyzer lands on `index=power` with `sourcetype="panel:phase"`; verify `props.conf` extracts `phase_a_amps`, `phase_b_amps`, `phase_c_amps`, `panel_id`, and `rated_amps` at search time.
• Deploy collectors on a Heavy Forwarder class host: SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app ID 1537) for SNMP/OID polling, optionally Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app ID 6266) for trap heavy sites, or an HEC endpoint in `inputs.conf` receiving JSON from a DCIM exporter—document the exact stanza names in your runbook.
• Align device clocks to NTP so `_time` matches the PQ dashboard; use Interactive Search over `-24h@h` to confirm numeric fields are populated before alerting.
• RBAC: grant `facility_power_rw` roles search/filter rights limited to `index=power` and schedule_saved_search capability only on the dedicated svc_facilities_scheduler service account.

Step 1 — Configure thresholds
Baseline each `panel_id` with actual rated frame amps (`rated_amps`) sourced from CMDB or asset tags; codify imbalance allowance (example catalog default 15%) in a macro `panel_imbalance_pct` so operators tune without rewriting SPL.

Step 2 — Build and schedule the detection
Paste the UC SPL into Search, validate skewed versus balanced panels, then save as alert `cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *` with `dispatch.earliest_time=-5m` and `dispatch.latest_time=now` so oscillations surface within one polling cadence.

Step 3 — Validate against native tooling
Compare Splunk-derived imbalance rows with the PQ analyzer UI or parallel SNMP poll on the same interval; reconcile differences caused by rounding before enabling paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish paired panels on a Dashboard Studio board (phase amp bars plus imbalance table), wire alert actions to email or webhook per facility policy, log tuning decisions referencing `panel_id` thresholds, and rehearse suppression macros keyed on planned maintenance CSV lookups.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: During rack migration CHG-88421, minute-level events for panel_id PDU-B-04 report phase_c_amps at 210 A, phase_a_amps at 140 A, phase_b_amps at 135 A, rated_amps at 800 A, and no sensor_fault flag; two consecutive `*/5 * * * *` runs exceed the imbalance macro so the saved search returns the row and the alert action emails facilities with the attached result set.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A cool-row event raises all three phases together by eighteen amps within one minute, the max-min spread stays under the macro limit, and the PQ analyzer shows a balanced neutral—searches complete with sub-threshold imbalance and no webhook fires.

evidence: Archive the alert artifact or scheduled export showing `_time`, `panel_id`, per-phase amps, computed `imbalance`, and screenshot or export from the PQ analyzer for the identical window plus the service request that documents any load change.
exclusions: Does not replace infrared thermography, certified arc-flash studies, or National Electrical Code compliance audits—only operational visibility on already-modeled `panel:phase` telemetry and Splunk-side thresholds.
